workflow.name: Sync NetSuite inventory items between NetSuite and Salesforce products
workflow.description:
## Short Description

Automatically exports inventory items records from NetSuite and syncs them into Salesforce — creating or updating Products.

## Who is this for / Use case

This template is designed for businesses and integration developers who:

- Use NetSuite as their ERP or CRM system.
- Need to automatically sync products data into Salesforce.
- Want a reliable, scalable solution that supports pagination and upserts.
- Prefer a ready-to-use, easily customizable workflow built on n8n.

## How it works

Initialize pagination offset.
1. Fetch Salesforce Pricebook values data.
2. Initialize pagination offset.
3. Fetch NetSuite list of Inventory Items.
4. Split the retrieved array into individual items.
5. Get details for each Inventory Item from NetSuite.
6. Find certain price value fetched from NetSuite
7. Upserts Product into Salesforce
8. Repeat until all records are processed, then end the workflow.

## Setup / Configuration

1. Import the workflow into your n8n instance.
2. Set your NetSuite and Salesforce credentials.
3. Ensure your Salesforce Account/Contact objects have a matching External ID field.
4. (Optional) Adjust mapping fields or filters to your data needs.
5. Execute manually or trigger on a schedule (e.g., daily sync).

parameters.content:
### This n8n template demonstrates how to export NetSuite Inventory Items and Upsert into Salesforce as Products.

### How it works
* Workflow processes 20 records per iteration until there are no records left.
* To find unique records - NetSuite Internal Id is used in Salesforce as External Id.


### How to use
* Set up NetSuite and Salesforce connections.
* The 'Execute Workflow Daily' node is used as an example but feel free to replace it with any other trigger.
* Run the flow manually to process all records. Non-existing records will be created, all existing - updated.
* After running workflow manually - you can set up schedule to run the flow. To process only records updated after last workflow run date - switch 'NS: Inventory Item - Get list of All records' node with 'NS: Inventory Item - Get Delta records'


### Requirements
* Salesforce connection working and External Id field exists.
* NetSuite connection working. Please refer to the details provided in [NetSuite REST Node description](https://www.npmjs.com/package/n8n-nodes-netsuite-rest).

### Note
* When running a workflow with Schedule trigger - on a first run workflow will use {today} date as a last export date.
* Currently workflow uses NetSuite REST community node   and can run on self-hosted n8n instance only.

### Need Help?
Feel free to reach out to support@entechsolutions.com

Happy Integrating!

## Salesforce section 2

### Records are inserted/updated into Salesforce here

## Netsuite section

### Records are pulled from NetSuite here

### Fell free to use filter with Q parameter passed in 'NS: Inventory Item - Get list of All records' step

Read about using Q parameter in [NetSuite Docs](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_1545222128.html)

### Note
* In this workflow only one Netsuite price level value is used

## Run for Delta records

### Replace a 'NS: Inventory Item - Get list of All records' node with a node below to run flow only for records changed after Last Workflow Run

nodes.name: NS: Inventory Item - Get Delta records
NS: Inventory Item - Get list of All records

parameters.content:
## Salesforce section 1

### Salesforce Pricebook values are fetched here

### Note
* In this workflow only one Salesforce Pricebook value is used

main.node: NS: Inventory Item - Get list of All records

user.name: Entech Solutions
user.bio:
Based in New York and New Jersey, ENTech Solutions is a digital consultancy specializing in high-velocity .NET and Azure engineering. Since 2006, we’ve applied a "Build Often, Ship Often" Agile approach to deliver rapid ROI.

We bridge traditional enterprise software with modern Agentic AI, integrating autonomous workflows and predictive analytics into core business processes. From OTA-compliant travel tech to scalable cloud apps, we turn complex specs into intelligent solutions.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
